warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui reactivity indices of furan were computed using **DFT (B3LYP/def2-TZVP)**, tier `standard`, in PySCF software. The calculation was carried out in the **gas phase** (no solvent model), assuming charge 0 and singlet multiplicity. Geometry was taken from the file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz`, with coordinates used as-is. The most electrophilic site determined using the Fukui function f⁺ was **C5**, with f⁺ = 1.9242931. Similarly, the most nucleophilic site determined using f⁻ was also **C5**, with f⁻ = 0.1616371. **SCF convergence succeeded**, and no grave issues were flagged, though some routine checks issued general warnings. Key warning messages included: "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)", "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)", and "common header present". Calculations passed all integrity tests; status: trustworthy. Data and plots were written to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_gpt-4o/20260703-215345_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
